 {"id":"wa-upg.11.3","title":"Add wa query/search explain command (TTY + --format json)","description":"# Task: Add search explain command

## Goal
Expose explainability via CLI/robot surfaces.

## Requirements
- Command options:
  - `wa query --explain "<query>" [--pane <id>]`
  - OR `wa search explain ...` (choose best UX)
- Output:
  - TTY: concise explanation + suggested next commands
  - JSON: structured explain results (reasons + evidence)

## Testing
- CLI contract tests for JSON schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can quickly understand why search misses and what to do.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:50:52.584250897Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T06:02:58.310736071Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T06:02:58.310666552Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-storage","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.11.3","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":111,"issue_id":"wa-upg.11.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added --explain flag to wa search command. When used, queries storage for panes, indexing stats, gaps, retention events, and time ranges, then runs the search explain engine to produce a ranked diagnostic. Supports both plain text and --format json output. Also added 3 new StorageHandle methods: get_gaps(), get_retention_cleanup_count(), get_segment_time_range(). All 38 wa tests and 1140 wa-core tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T06:02:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.11.4","title":"Improve FTS errors (invalid syntax, scopes) with actionable remediation","description":"# Task: Improve FTS errors

## Goal
Make FTS errors actionable and consistent.

## Requirements
- Invalid syntax errors:
  - stable error code
  - show where parsing failed (when safe)
  - suggest simpler query or escaping
- Scope issues:
  - clearly explain when query was limited by pane/time/retention
- Integrate with explain engine when possible.

## Testing
- Unit tests for error codes and messages.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Search failures feel self-diagnosing.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacMeadow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:51:02.460885617Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:05.520272345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:05.520138166Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-storage","area-tests","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.11.4","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":107,"issue_id":"wa-upg.11.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented structured FTS error handling. Added FtsErrorKind enum (UnbalancedQuotes, InvalidOperator, EmptyQuery, Syntax) with Display impl. New StorageError::FtsQuerySyntax variant carries structured kind + original query. Pre-validation in validate_fts_query catches empty queries and unbalanced quotes before hitting SQLite. SQLite errors categorized by message content. CLI handler uses pattern matching (classify_search_error, print_search_error_hint) instead of string detection. JSON errors include stable E_FTS_SYNTAX code and category-specific hints. Plain text shows tailored hints per error kind with examples. Error code WA-2021 registered for FtsQuerySyntax. Added 5 unit tests for validation (empty, whitespace, unbalanced quotes, balanced quotes, valid queries). Updated existing tests. All 1094 wa-core + 38 wa tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.11.5","title":"Tests: search explain reasons and evidence (fixtures)","description":"# Task: Tests for search explain

## Goal
Lock in explain behavior.

## Requirements
- Fixture-based tests for each reason:
  - excluded pane
  - index lag
  - retention pruned
  - invalid syntax
  - recent GAP
- Assert:
  - reason IDs
  - evidence fields present
  - suggested commands present

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests make explain output changes intentional.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:51:11.433601094Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T05:56:42.556437583Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T05:56:42.556371550Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-storage","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.11.5","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":110,"issue_id":"wa-upg.11.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"11 fixture-based unit tests in search_explain::tests covering all 8 check functions: empty database, excluded pane, unknown pane, FTS inconsistency, gaps, retention cleanup, stale panes, narrow time range, healthy system, confidence sorting, and plain rendering.","created_at":"2026-01-29T05:56:38Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-upg.11.6","title":"E2E: search explain scenarios (excluded pane, index lag, invalid syntax)","description":"# Task: E2E search explain scenarios

## Goal
Validate explainability in a real-ish runtime.

## Scenarios
1) Excluded pane:
- configure exclude rule
- produce output
- search explain should indicate exclusion

2) Index lag:
- simulate slow index updates/backpressure
- explain indicates lag and last indexed seq/time

3) Invalid syntax:
- run an invalid query
- error code + remediation + explain hints

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include outputs and logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E proves explain output is useful and deterministic.

 {"id":"wa-upg.11.7","title":"Docs: search explain and indexing lag troubleshooting","description":"# Task: Document search explain

## Goal
Help users interpret explain output and troubleshoot search issues.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - common reasons and what they mean
  - how to fix indexing lag
  - how to adjust pane include/exclude
  - safe diagnostics steps

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can self-diagnose most search issues.

 {"id":"wa-upg.12","title":"[EPIC] Backpressure-aware watcher (bounded queues + graceful degradation)","description":"# [EPIC] Backpressure-aware watcher

## Mission
Ensure the watcher remains stable under load by:
- enforcing bounded queues everywhere
- adapting polling/work under backpressure
- degrading safely (explicit GAP) rather than deadlocking or OOMing

## Why This Matters
Backpressure problems are the #1 way background daemons become unreliable:
- memory grows
- latency spikes
- the system "hangs" and users lose trust

## Scope
- Define backpressure signals and thresholds.
- Implement adaptive behavior:
  - slow down polling when queues are deep
  - batch work
  - emit health warnings
  - record GAP events when continuity can't be guaranteed
- Ensure health output makes backpressure obvious.

## Success Criteria
- Under sustained load, watcher stays responsive and memory-bounded.
- Backpressure behavior is visible and explainable.

## Testing
- Integration tests with slow consumers.
- E2E stress scenario that triggers backpressure and verifies graceful behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Watcher has explicit backpressure handling and no unbounded buffers.
- Tests and E2E validate behavior and produce good artifacts.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:51:48.333972506Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:51:48.333972506Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-daemon","area-ingest","area-reliability","area-tests","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12","depends_on_id":"wa-upg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.12.1","title":"Design: backpressure signals, thresholds, and degradation policy","description":"# Task: Design backpressure handling

## Goal
Specify backpressure signals and the system's response policy.

## Requirements
- Signals:
  - ingest queue depth
  - storage writer queue depth
  - detection pipeline lag
  - indexing lag
- Thresholds:
  - warning level
  - critical level
- Responses:
  - adaptive polling slowdown
  - batching
  - health warnings
  - explicit GAP insertion when continuity uncertain

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy is deterministic and implementable.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:52:00.083318245Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:15:47.719177609Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-daemon","area-design","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
